name: Tarot spread ob3ect: the 78-card tarot deck as a self-imscribing divination system. FSPLIT: the Celtic Cross spread splits the querent's situation into ten positional meanings — present, challenge, past, future, above, below, advice, external influences, hopes/fears, outcome. FFUSE: the reader synthesizes all ten positions back into a coherent narrative that reconstitutes the original question with insight. VINIT: the shuffled but undrawn deck. TANCH: the completed spread layout. AFWD: a card drawn upright. AREV: a card drawn reversed. ENGAGR: two cards in the same spread that flatly contradict each other yet must both be true — the Tower and the Star together.

phase_0.domain_name: Tarot spread ob3ect: the 78-card tarot deck as a self-imscribing divination system. FSPLIT: the Celtic Cross spread splits the querent's situation into ten positional meanings — present, challenge, past, future, above, below, advice, external influences, hopes/fears, outcome. FFUSE: the reader synthesizes all ten positions back into a coherent narrative that reconstitutes the original question with insight. VINIT: the shuffled but undrawn deck. TANCH: the completed spread layout. AFWD: a card drawn upright. AREV: a card drawn reversed. ENGAGR: two cards in the same spread that flatly contradict each other yet must both be true — the Tower and the Star together.

VINIT.justification: void/uninitialized state before any card is placed

TANCH.justification: terminal anchor that contains the whole reading

AFWD.justification: forward morphism toward a target meaning

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: the reader's self-recognition of their own interpretation

FSPLIT.justification: splits the querent's situation into ten positional meanings

FFUSE.justification: reconstitutes all ten positions into a coherent narrative

EVALF.chosen_element: a blocked or reversed card meaning

ENGAGR.chosen_element: the Tower and the Star together in the same spread
ENGAGR.justification: paradox: both flatly contradictory yet both must be true

IFIX.chosen_element: the written record of the reading
IFIX.justification: permanent irreversible fixation of the interpretation

phase_3.void_description: shuffled deck before any card is drawn
phase_3.true_description: a card meaning that is clearly affirmative
phase_3.false_description: a card meaning that is clearly negative
phase_3.both_description: the Tower and the Star together — destruction and hope simultaneously

steps.domain_action: Step 1 IMSCRIB: the reader acknowledges their own role as interpreter
Step 2 AREV: the reader shuffles the deck (descent into randomness)
Step 3 FSPLIT: the reader lays out the Celtic Cross spread (ten positions)
Step 4 AFWD: the reader interprets each card upright (forward meaning)
Step 5 FFUSE: the reader synthesizes all positions into one narrative
Step 6 CLINK: the reader links the narrative to the original question
Step 7 IFIX: the reader records the interpretation in a journal
Step 8 IMSCRIB: the reader recognizes the reading as a coherent whole

phase_5.compiler_frontend: the reader's intuition and knowledge of tarot
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: the positional relationships between cards in the spread
phase_5.memory_mechanism: the reader's memory and the written journal
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: the order of positions in the Celtic Cross (1 to 10)
phase_5.alfs_store: the Rider-Waite-Smith tarot deck as canonical reference

phase_6.cycle_cost: the reader's mental energy per reading
phase_6.post_cycle_state: spread laid out, narrative formed, insight gained
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the reading reorganizes existing information without net disorder
